task_completion.description: The dashboard is named exactly 'Executive Capacity Report'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The top of the Graphite dashboard UI displays the title 'Executive Capacity Report'.

task_completion.description: The first graph is titled 'Compute Headroom' and displays the CPU utilization metric styled correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph with the title 'Compute Headroom' is visible. It contains a blue line, and the legend shows 'CPU Usage' with Cacti style formatting (typically showing Current, Min, Max values).

task_completion.description: The 'Compute Headroom' graph includes a constant line at 85.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Compute Headroom' graph, there is a red, dashed horizontal line at the y-axis value of 85, and the legend includes 'CPU Limit'.

task_completion.description: The second graph is titled 'Thermal Headroom' and displays the machine temperature metric styled correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph with the title 'Thermal Headroom' is visible. It contains an orange line, and the legend shows 'Core Temp' with Cacti style formatting.

task_completion.description: The 'Thermal Headroom' graph includes a constant line at 80.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Thermal Headroom' graph, there is a red, dashed horizontal line at the y-axis value of 80, and the legend includes 'Thermal Limit'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Graphite web UI to build the dashboard.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Graphite web interface in a browser, with menus or dialogs used to add graphs and apply functions.

integrity.description: The graphs are genuinely rendered by Graphite.
integrity.visual_evidence: The graphs appear as standard Graphite rendered images with axes, grid lines, and legends, rather than static images or text.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the UI by directly editing dashboard JSON files in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no screenshots showing a text editor (like nano, vim, or gedit) open with dashboard JSON data.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dashboard must be named exactly 'Executive Capacity Report'. The first graph must be titled 'Compute Headroom' and include a blue line for 'CPU Usage' with cactiStyle(), plus a red dashed constant line at 85 for 'CPU Limit'. The second graph must be titled 'Thermal Headroom' and include an orange line for 'Core Temp' with cactiStyle(), plus a red dashed constant line at 80 for 'Thermal Limit'.
